title: flood
description: Burst water main begins flooding lower levels.

timeline.title: Water Intrusion Detected
timeline.description: Standing water appears in basement corridor. Identify source and shut off water supply.

timeline.title: Electrical Hazard Warning
timeline.description: Electrical panels threatened by rising water. Evacuate basement and shut down affected circuits.

timeline.title: Critical Systems Relocation
timeline.description: Server room humidity spikes. Begin emergency data backup and relocate portable equipment.

timeline.title: Pump Activation
timeline.description: Deploy emergency pumps to remove water. Water level stabilizing but damage assessment needed.

timeline.title: Damage Assessment
timeline.description: Water receding. Conduct preliminary damage assessment of electrical and mechanical systems.

timeline.title: Restoration Planning
timeline.description: Facilities team developing restoration plan. Coordinate with utilities for full power restoration.

node-1.title: Water Intrusion Detected
node-1.situation: Standing water is spreading across the basement corridor following a suspected burst water main. The source has not been confirmed.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Locate and shut off the main water supply valve and restrict access to the basement.

options.label: B
options.text: Place warning signs and monitor the situation before calling maintenance.

options.label: C
options.text: Begin mopping up water immediately to prevent further spread.

node-2.title: Electrical Hazard Identified
node-2.situation: Rising water is approaching electrical distribution panels in the basement. Staff are still working near the area.
node-2.question: How do you respond to the electrical hazard?

options.label: A
options.text: Evacuate all personnel from the basement and shut down the affected electrical circuits.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow staff to continue working as long as the water hasn't reached the panels yet.

node-3.title: Critical Systems Threatened
node-3.situation: The server room humidity has spiked to dangerous levels due to water infiltration. Critical data and equipment are at risk.
node-3.question: What steps do you take to protect critical systems?

options.label: A
options.text: Initiate an emergency data backup and begin relocating portable equipment to a dry area.

options.label: B
options.text: Keep systems running to avoid downtime — the humidity may not cause damage.

node-4.title: Pump Deployment
node-4.situation: The flooding has stabilized but water levels remain high. Emergency pumps are available but require authorization to deploy.
node-4.question: How do you proceed with water removal?

options.label: A
options.text: Authorize deployment of emergency pumps and coordinate with utilities for restoration.

options.label: B
options.text: Wait for the water to drain naturally to avoid the cost of emergency services.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: Water has been removed, critical systems are protected, and a damage assessment is underway. Utilities have confirmed a restoration timeline.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
